subtitle: From Rebel Challenger to Lord of Pataliputra

panels.shot: low-angle heroic wide of a young Chandragupta on a hill overlooking the Gangetic plain
panels.action: Chandragupta Maurya stands on a rocky rise at dusk, spear in hand, cloak whipping in the wind, as distant cities and rivers of the Gangetic plain glow under a vast, hazy sky.

dialogue.text: In the age when Magadha’s Nandas ruled from mighty Pataliputra, a young war-leader named Chandragupta rose to bind the plains under the Peacock Throne.

i18n.hi: जब मगध के नंदा शक्तिशाली पटलिपुत्र से राज करते थे, तब चंद्रगुप्त नाम का युवा सेनापति उठा, जिसने मैदानों को मोरासन तले जोड़ा।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of the Gangetic plain and Pataliputra’s fortifications
panels.action: A sweeping view of the Gangetic plain shows rivers curling around fertile fields and the long wooden palisades and watchtowers of Pataliputra stretching beside the Ganga.

dialogue.text: In the fourth century before the Common Era, Magadha’s power rose on the eastern plains, its capital Pataliputra a long wooden fortress hugging the Ganga.

i18n.hi: ईसा से चार सौ साल पहले, पूरब के मैदानों पर मगध उभरा, राजधानी पटलिपुत्र गंगा किनारे लंबा लकड़ी का किला था।

panels.shot: interior wide of a Nanda court scene, seen from the back of the hall
panels.action: Inside a grand hall, a richly adorned Nanda king reclines on a raised platform, surrounded by officials and guards, while emissaries bow on polished floors under flickering lamps.

dialogue.text: The Nandas commanded wealth and elephants, and many smaller rulers bowed to their court, yet their riches bred fear as much as loyalty.

i18n.hi: नंदाओं के पास दौलत और हाथी थे, छोटे शासक दरबार में झुकते थे, पर उनकी संपत्ति डर भी बढ़ाती थी, वफादारी नहीं।

panels.shot: medium shot of a young Chandragupta in a modest rural setting
panels.action: A lean, youthful Chandragupta in simple warrior’s garb trains with a spear in a dusty yard near a thatched dwelling, watched by a few older villagers by a riverbank.

dialogue.text: Later traditions place Chandragupta’s boyhood in modest surroundings, a youth of uncertain lineage, shaped more by hard soil and drill than by palace life.

i18n.hi: कहानी है कि चंद्रगुप्त का बचपन साधारण था, खानदान धुंधला, मिट्टी और मशक्कत ने उसे ज्यादा गढ़ा, महलों ने कम।

panels.shot: close-up on Chandragupta’s face as he listens to travellers
panels.action: Chandragupta sits near a village fire at night, eyes intent, listening as a caravan leader gestures toward the dark, speaking of distant cities and armies.

dialogue.text: By the fire he heard of Magadhan taxes, of Nanda hoards, of men crushed between greedy rulers and marching armies, and his anger grew quiet and cold.

i18n.hi: आग के पास वह सुनता टैक्स, नंदा के खजाने, लालची राजाओं और मार्च करती फौजों तले पिसते लोगों की बातें… और उसका गुस्सा ठंडा, गहरा होता गया।

panels.shot: medium shot in a small gathering of armed followers in a grove
panels.action: In a shady grove, Chandragupta stands before a circle of rough-clad warriors and clan leaders, his hand raised as he speaks, their faces lit with wary hope.

dialogue.text: He drifted into the world of war-bands and clans, where strong hands and clear plans drew followers, and his tongue learned to promise a different order.

i18n.hi: धीरे‑धीरे वह लड़ाका टोलियों और कुलों की दुनिया में बह गया, जहां ताकत और साफ योजना पर लोग जुटते थे, और उसकी ज़बान ने नए निज़ाम के वादे करना सीख लिया।

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle of Chandragupta turned away from Pataliputra in frustration
panels.action: Chandragupta, dust-covered, stands outside distant city walls, barred by armed gatekeepers, turning away with clenched fists toward the open land beyond.

dialogue.text: Traditions remember him first rebuffed by power, kept at the gate of courts and camps, his ambitions larger than the petty commands offered to him.

i18n.hi: परंपरा कहती है कि शुरू में सत्ता ने उसे धक्का ही दिया, दरबारों और छावनियों के फाटक पर ही रोके रखा, उसकी हिम्मत उन छोटे पदों से कहीं बड़ी थी जो उसे दिए जा रहे थे।

panels.shot: wide shot of a northern landscape with distant foreign-style encampment
panels.action: On a rocky northern plain, Chandragupta stands with a few companions, looking toward a distant camp of foreign soldiers in long cloaks and helmets near a river.

dialogue.text: To the northwest, beyond the Indus, another force had shaken the land: Alexander’s Macedonian armies, whose march stirred both awe and resistance.

i18n.hi: उत्तर‑पश्चिम में, सिंधु के पार, एक और तूफान उठा था: सिकंदर की मकदूनियाई सेनाएं, जिनकी चढ़ाई ने डर भी जगाया, और विरोध भी।

panels.shot: close-up on Chandragupta in conversation with a Greek soldier in a market lane
panels.action: In a bustling frontier bazaar, Chandragupta leans in conversation with a bearded Greek soldier in a cloak, while traders and pack animals squeeze past them.

dialogue.text: Greek writers later recalled an Indian youth, Androcottus, who moved among their garrisons, weighing the strength and limits of these new arrivals.

i18n.hi: यूनानी लेखक एक भारतीय युवक, अंद्रोकॉट्टोस, को याद करते हैं, जो उनकी छावनियों में घूमता, इन नए फौजियों की ताकत और कमजोरियां तौलता था।

panels.shot: medium shot of Chandragupta addressing his men at night around a fire
panels.action: Around a campfire under a starry sky, Chandragupta stands with a cloak around his shoulders, addressing a tight circle of armed men whose faces glow in the firelight.

dialogue.text: Magadha is rich but brittle. Strike the heart, and the limbs will fall.

i18n.hi: "मगध धनवान है, पर नाज़ुक। दिल पर मारो, हाथ‑पैर खुद गिरेंगे।"

panels.shot: bird’s-eye view of scattered skirmishes in the countryside
panels.action: From above, small bands of Chandragupta’s warriors harry supply caravans and tax-collectors along forested roads, while distant smoke rises over scattered outposts.

dialogue.text: He did not rush Pataliputra. He first cut at its reach, testing Nanda garrisons, loosening the grip of the king on distant lands.

i18n.hi: उसने पटलिपुत्र पर झपट्टा नहीं मारा। पहले उसने उसकी पकड़ काटी, नंदा चौकियों को आज़माया, दूर के इलाकों पर राजा की पकड़ ढीली की।

panels.shot: wide shot of Chandragupta negotiating with a regional ruler in a tent
panels.action: Inside a large campaign tent, Chandragupta sits across from a local ruler in fine robes, a map spread between them, while their attendants watch in wary silence.

dialogue.text: Around him, minor rulers and chiefs judged the winds. Some he humbled in battle, others he bound with terms that promised order instead of chaos.

i18n.hi: इधर‑उधर छोटे राजा‑मालिक हवा भांपते रहे। कुछ को उसने युद्ध में झुकाया, कुछ को ऐसे समझौते में बांधा जो अराजकता की जगह कड़ा निज़ाम देते थे।

panels.shot: medium shot of Chandragupta in counsel with strategists, scrolls and maps around
panels.action: In a lamplit shelter, Chandragupta leans over a rough map on a wooden board, markers placed on river crossings and roads, as seasoned captains point and argue.

dialogue.text: Indian tradition later gave this cunning a single name, Chanakya, yet no court record survives; what remains clear is that Chandragupta’s campaigns were guided by careful calculation.

i18n.hi: भारतीय परंपरा इस चालाकी को एक नाम देती है – चाणक्य – लेकिन कोई दरबारी लेख नहीं बचा; बस इतना साफ है कि चंद्रगुप्त की लड़ाइयां ठोस सोच से चलाई गईं।

panels.shot: epic wide shot of Chandragupta’s army on the march toward Pataliputra
panels.action: A vast column of infantry, cavalry and elephants moves along a dusty road toward the horizon, with Chandragupta riding at the front, Pataliputra’s palisades faint in the distance.

dialogue.text: When his base was firm and his enemies divided, he turned the full weight of his army east, toward the long wooden walls of Pataliputra.

i18n.hi: जब उसका आधार मजबूत हो गया और दुश्मन बंट गए, तब उसने अपनी पूरी सेना पूरब घुमाई, पटलिपुत्र की लंबी लकड़ी की दीवारों की ओर।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of Chandragupta before Pataliputra’s walls in battle formation
panels.action: Outside the towering palisades, Chandragupta stands beside a war elephant, shield raised, as ranks of soldiers and elephants form lines behind him in the haze of churned dust.

dialogue.text: Hold the lines. Break their will, and the city will open.

i18n.hi: "पंक्ति संभालो। उनका हौसला तोड़ो, शहर खुद दरवाज़ा खोल देगा।"

panels.shot: dynamic wide shot hinting at siege and maneuver without gore
panels.action: Ladders rise against the outer defenses as defenders hurl down missiles; elsewhere, Chandragupta directs a flanking force toward a less-guarded gate amid swirling dust and confusion.

dialogue.text: No detailed chronicle describes the fall, only that the Nanda king yielded and fled, and Magadha’s heart passed into Chandragupta’s hands.

i18n.hi: विस्तार से कोई इतिहास नहीं बताता, बस इतना कि नंदा राजा हार मानकर भागा, और मगध का दिल चंद्रगुप्त के हाथ आ गया।

panels.shot: interior wide of Chandragupta entering the Nanda palace
panels.action: Chandragupta strides into the pillared hall of the conquered palace, armor dusty, as Nanda officials bow or watch nervously from the shadows, the old throne standing empty before him.

dialogue.text: In Pataliputra’s halls, where Nanda wealth had glittered, a new master walked, and the city’s scribes bent their records to a fresh name.

i18n.hi: पटलिपुत्र की उन दीवारों में, जहां कभी नंदा की दौलत चमकती थी, अब नया स्वामी चलता था, और शहर के लेखक अपने लेखों में नया नाम गढ़ रहे थे।

panels.shot: symbolic medium shot of Chandragupta adopting the peacock standard
panels.action: On a high terrace, Chandragupta raises a tall standard topped with a stylized peacock emblem, while soldiers and courtiers below watch in silence against the broad river backdrop.

dialogue.text: Later memory linked his house to the peacock, Maurya to Moriya, though the exact path of that symbol lies in layered tradition rather than clear record.

i18n.hi: बाद की याद ने उसके कुल को मोर से जोड़ा, मौर्य को मोरिया से, लेकिन इस निशान का असली रास्ता साफ इतिहास से ज्यादा परतदार परंपरा में छिपा है।

panels.shot: expansive bird’s-eye shot of the Mauryan realm radiating from Pataliputra
panels.action: From high above, roads and rivers radiate from Pataliputra across the Gangetic plain toward the northwest, dotted with forts, towns, and marching columns bearing Chandragupta’s standard.

dialogue.text: From his new capital he spread his rule over Magadha and beyond, forging one of the subcontinent’s first great empires from the Indus to the eastern seas.

i18n.hi: नई राजधानी से उसने मगध और उससे आगे तक राज फैलाया, सिंधु से पूरब के समुंदर तक उपमहाद्वीप के पहले बड़े साम्राज्यों में एक गढ़ा।

panels.shot: medium shot of Chandragupta in later years conferring with an envoy in Greek dress
panels.action: An older Chandragupta, robed but still broad-shouldered, meets a Greek envoy in a courtyard, both men gesturing over a carved map while attendants stand at a respectful distance.

dialogue.text: Greek accounts remembered him again as the Indian king who faced Seleucus, trading territory and elephants, and fixing a new balance between western and eastern powers.

i18n.hi: यूनानी किताबें उसे फिर याद करती हैं, उस भारतीय राजा के रूप में जिसने सेल्युकस का सामना किया, जमीन और हाथियों का सौदा किया और पूरब‑पश्चिम की ताकतों में नया संतुलन तय किया।

panels.shot: contemplative medium shot of Chandragupta looking out over Pataliputra at sunset
panels.action: At sunset on a palace balcony, Chandragupta stands alone, hands resting on the stone rail, watching the Ganga gleam and the city hum below under an orange sky.

dialogue.text: Behind the legends, what remains certain is this: a determined war-leader seized Magadha, raised a new standard, and showed how courage bound to discipline could remake the map of an age.

i18n.hi: किंवदंतियों के पीछे बस इतना पक्का है: एक अडिग सेनानायक ने मगध छीन लिया, नया ध्वज फहराया, और दिखा दिया कि अनुशासन से बंधी हिम्मत एक पूरे युग का नक्शा बदल सकती है।

title_i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त ने मोर वाला ध्वज उठाया

subtitle_i18n.en: From Rebel Challenger to Lord of Pataliputra
subtitle_i18n.hi: विद्रोही से पटलिपुत्र के स्वामी तक
